document_summary: This document covers topics such as data, model, eeg, seizure.

top_terms.context: The goal of this research is to develop a deep learning binary classification model capable of detecting anomalies in EEG segments from pediatric comatose patients that are indicative of epileptic seizures.
According to all three reviews, the most common approach in deep learning model architectures for EEG analysis is by far a convolutional neural network (CNN).

Research shows there are various approaches in applying deep learning for EEG analysis. Insights from literature study can be used to answer the first sub-question: Which deep learning model architecture is most suitable for identifying the relevant patterns and features in an EEG that are indicative of an epileptic seizure?

Research shows there are various approaches in applying deep learning for EEG analysis. Insights from literature study can be used to answer the first sub-question: Which deep learning model architecture is most suitable for identifying the relevant patterns and features in an EEG that are indicative of an epileptic seizure?

Three sub-questions were formulated to navigate and provide an answer to the following main question: How can deep learning be applied to minimally processed EEG data for the detection of epileptic seizures in pediatric comatose patients?
Neurologists are extensively trained to recognize abnormalities in an EEG pattern, but it is possible that not all indicators are known yet.
For seizure detection tasks the most common architectures are CNN, RNN and CNN-RNN combinations.

This refers to the dimension of the input data used. Architectures with 2D-CNN layers are mostly used for tasks involving images, which is why in EEG analysis the signals tend to first be converted into an image representation as a spectrogram, as shown in Figure 3.
It is mentioned that models with 2D-CNN architectures, followed by 1D-CNN models, are most commonly used for seizure detection.
There is a strong preference for using minimally processed data to explore if deep learning is able to learn patterns from (limited) data on its own.

scope of this research is set to medically-induced comatose patients in Erasmuc MC's Pediatric Intensive Care Unit. Research is carried out with a selection of data from such patients, consisting of 127 EEG recordings. These are specifically selected for this project by a skilled neurologist based on their availability, qu

results_or_conclusions: The goal of this research is to develop a deep learning binary classification model capable of detecting anomalies in EEG segments from pediatric comatose patients that are indicative of epileptic seizures.
There is a strong preference for using minimally processed data to explore if deep learning is able to learn patterns from (limited) data on its own.
possible_value_for_knowledge_platform: This document provides insights into the application of deep learning in EEG analysis, specifically focusing on seizure detection in comatose pediatric patients. It also highlights the importance of minimally processed data and different neural network architectures.
